message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_mode composes through gather for autograd [bd-1tax]

tensor_mode was severed-autograd: it extracted tensor_values, ran
the sort + count-most-frequent loop in plain f64, and rebuilt
fresh requires_grad=false leaves for both values and indices —
silently dropping gradients. PyTorch's torch.mode is differentiable
through the values output (the indices are intrinsically integer
and stay non-differentiable).

Composition (autograd-aware values output):

  1. Compute the per-outer-slice mode position in plain f64 (the
     sort + count-runs is non-differentiable; only the position
     matters for the autograd graph).
  2. Build a same-rank index tensor selecting that position along
     the last dim (size 1 there, matching tensor_gather's contract).
  3. tensor_gather(input, last_dim, index) extracts the value and
     keeps it on the autograd tape.
  4. tensor_squeeze drops the trailing size-1 dim (rank >= 2 case);
     rank-1 input keeps shape [1] to match the pre-fix contract.

Backward through gather scatter-adds the incoming gradient at
whichever input position holds the mode value — exactly the
analytical adjoint.

Forward also switches the per-run-length comparison from
`(a - b).abs() < f64::EPSILON` to IEEE `==`, matching the
tensor_unique parity fix (3b73d8d). Distinct subnormals like
5e-324 vs 1e-323 are no longer fused into a single run.

Test: mode_propagates_gradient_to_winning_position asserts that on
input [1, 2, 2, 3] the backward of sum(mode(x).0) deposits exactly
one nonzero gradient (= 1.0) at a position holding the mode value.

All 5 existing mode tests (mode_1d, mode_single_element, mode_2d,
mode_scalar_errors, mode_zero_last_dim_errors) continue to pass —
forward parity preserved bit-for-bit on the integer-spaced cases.

Files closure for the mode slice of frankentorch-1tax.

Workspace: 1129 ft-api tests green (1128 + 1 new), clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
